title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Runtime Lens: Counter Update
summary: A counting loop resets existing entries, aliases a shared dictionary, includes empty tokens, sorts while mutating, and never reconciles token totals with frequency totals. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the counter update increment in inspectable frequency engine. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-counter-update — Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_frequency_counter_2(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-counter-update-1hsg1t
# Competency: bookbot-counter-update.
# Case 6203: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_counter_update_1hsg1t(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1hsg1t = "1hsg1t-t1gsh1-6203"
    assert evidence_variant_1hsg1t
    evidence_axes_1hsg1t = (
        "corpus1hsg1t",
        "policyt1gsh1",
        "revision6203",
        "command1hsg1t6203",
        "reportt1gsh16203",
        "test62031hsg1t",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1hsg1t)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Counter Update model · Theory Counter Update, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Counter and an ordinary dictionary behave identically for every operation.
